findings.explanation: „Nieten“ can mean the technique of riveting, but also rivets as physical metal items/products. In a puzzle that also has „Metallprodukte“, this is not fully unambiguous.
findings.suggestedFix: Use a clearer technique such as „Bohren“ or „Walzen“, or change the item to „Vernieten“.

findings.explanation: The categories „Webtechniken“, „Textilien“ and „Textil-Muster“ overlap semantically. „Damast“ and „Jacquard“ are not just simple visual patterns; they are tied to weave structures/techniques and can also name fabrics.
findings.suggestedFix: For an easy puzzle, use plain visual patterns only, e.g. „Streifen, Karo, Punkte, Blumenmuster“, and avoid weave/fabric terms.

findings.explanation: „Faden“ and „Garn“ are very close everyday terms and may be treated as near-synonyms by solvers, especially at easy tier.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace one with a more distinct textile item such as „Band“, „Filz“ or „Spitze“.

findings.explanation: „Leinen“ is commonly the fabric/material made from flax, not the fiber itself. As a „Faserart“, „Flachs“ would be clearer.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace „Leinen“ with „Flachs“.

findings.explanation: „Leinwandbindung“, „Köperbindung“ and „Atlasbindung“ are specialist textile terms and feel too technical for an easy everyday German puzzle.
findings.suggestedFix: Use more concrete textile actions such as „Weben“, „Nähen“, „Stricken“, „Sticken“.

findings.explanation: „Bundeswirtschaftsrat“ is not a standard German economic institution on the level of Bundesbank, IHK or Ifo-Institut. It is likely to confuse solvers or be perceived as invented/incorrect.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace with a clearer institution such as „Bundeskartellamt“, „Wirtschaftsministerium“ or „DIHK“.

findings.explanation: This puzzle is heavy with institutions, abbreviations and macroeconomic concepts. For easy tier, „BIP“, „IHK“, „Ifo-Institut“, „Tarifvertrag“ and „Mittelstand“ are relatively abstract compared with concrete everyday categories.
findings.suggestedFix: Move to medium or simplify with more everyday items.

findings.explanation: „Frankfurt“ and „Hamburg“ are not especially characteristic easy-recognition German Maschinenbau locations in the way Stuttgart is. The group feels arbitrary and weakly anchored.
findings.suggestedFix: Use more clearly associated industrial locations or replace the whole category with a more concrete one.

findings.explanation: „Maschinenbau-Produkte“ and „Maschinenbau-Branchen“ overlap: „Werkzeugbau“ and „Fördertechnik“ can be understood as product/technology areas within machinery rather than cleanly separate concepts.
findings.suggestedFix: Make one category concrete products only and the other clearly company/region/person categories.

findings.explanation: „Boehringer“ is not normally used alone as the company name; the recognizable company is „Boehringer Ingelheim“.
findings.suggestedFix: Use „Boehringer Ingelheim“.

findings.explanation: „Pharma-Forschung“ contains process/regulatory/medical evaluation terms, not just research fields. „Zulassung“, „Wirksamkeit“ and „Nebenwirkung“ are broader pharma or medical terms, which overlaps with „Wirkstoffgruppen“ and general pharma vocabulary.
findings.suggestedFix: Rename to „Pharma-Begriffe“ or use clearer research items such as „Laborversuch“, „Doppelblindstudie“, „Wirkstoffsuche“, „Placebo“.

findings.explanation: „Wind“ as an energy carrier and „Windrad“ as an energy technology are tightly coupled and may feel like the same concept split across groups. This is not fatal, but it weakens separation.
findings.suggestedFix: Use „Windenergie“ for the carrier/source or replace one item with a less paired term.

findings.explanation: „Dessau“ is not an obvious or widely recognized German energy location at easy tier. The location category feels arbitrary compared with concrete energy companies and technologies.
findings.suggestedFix: Use more recognizable energy locations or replace the category.

findings.explanation: „Ökologisch“, „Konventionell“, „Bewässerung“ and „Pflanzenschutz“ are mixed levels: the first two are farming systems, while the latter two are practices that can occur within those systems.
findings.suggestedFix: Use one coherent concept, e.g. all farming systems or all field-work practices.

findings.explanation: „EU-Direktzahlungen“, „Bergbauernprämie“ and „Flächenprämie“ are policy/subsidy terms that are not everyday easy-tier vocabulary.
findings.suggestedFix: Move to medium or replace with concrete farm terms.

findings.explanation: „Bayern“ is both a tourism region and the location strongly associated with „Oktoberfest“. The categories are understandable, but this creates location/event cross-association.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace „Bayern“ with a more specific region such as „Allgäu“ or replace „Oktoberfest“ with a landmark.

findings.explanation: „Hausmannskost“ is more a type of food/cuisine than a neutral gastronomy term, so it can reasonably pull toward „Regionalküchen“ or food categories.
findings.suggestedFix: Use a clearer restaurant term such as „Reservierung“, „Bedienung“ or „Rechnung“.

findings.explanation: „Handelsprodukte“ is mislabeled: „Eigenmarke“, „Markenprodukt“, „Sonderangebot“ and „Aktionsware“ are product/offer types and retail concepts, overlapping with „Handelsbegriffe“.
findings.suggestedFix: Rename „Handelsprodukte“ to „Angebotsarten“ or replace with actual product categories like „Milch, Brot, Waschmittel, Obst“.

findings.explanation: „Verfassungsschutz“ is not a police authority; it is a domestic intelligence agency. In a „Polizeibehörden“ group, this is factually wrong.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace with „Zollfahndung“ only if broad law enforcement is intended, or use a police-specific authority.

findings.explanation: „Kripo“ is not simply a unit parallel to SEK/MEK/Bereitschaftspolizei; it is a police branch/department, which overlaps with police authority/organization concepts.
findings.suggestedFix: Use another clearly unit-like term or rename the category to „Polizeibereiche“.

findings.explanation: „Rettungswagen“ belongs primarily to rescue/EMS rather than firefighting, though fire departments may operate ambulances. In a category specifically labeled „Feuerwehr-Fahrzeuge“, it is ambiguous.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace with „Rüstwagen“ or „Einsatzleitwagen“.

findings.explanation: „Hydrant“ is infrastructure, not equipment carried by firefighters. It may be associated with firefighting but does not fit cleanly with Atemschutz, Schlauch and Schutzanzug.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace with „Helm“, „Axt“ or „Strahlrohr“.

findings.explanation: „Spezialkräfte“ overlaps with branch/unit categories because KSK belongs to the Heer and KSM belongs to the Marine. The intended split is understandable but not clean.
findings.suggestedFix: Use a non-overlapping category such as „Dienstgrade“ or „Ausrüstung“.

findings.explanation: „ABC-Abwehr“ is not generally classified as Spezialkräfte parallel to KSK/KSM/Fernspäher. It is a capability/troop type for CBRN defense.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace with a clearer special-operations item or rename the category.

findings.explanation: „Olaf Scholz“ is not a current party chair of the SPD, so placing him under „Parteivorsitzende“ is factually wrong.
findings.suggestedFix: Use a current or historically explicit SPD party chair, or rename the category to „Bekannte Politiker“.

findings.explanation: „Robert Habeck“ is not unambiguously a current party chair of Bündnis 90/Die Grünen. The item is better understood as a prominent politician than as a chairperson.
findings.suggestedFix: Use a current or historically explicit Green party chair, or rename the category to „Bekannte Politiker“.

findings.explanation: „SPD-Godesberger“ and „FDP-Neoliberalismus“ are awkward/advanced political-history clues for easy tier, and the category mixes events, ideological labels and shorthand.
findings.suggestedFix: Use clearer historical items such as „Godesberger Programm“ or move the puzzle to a higher tier.

findings.explanation: „Wahlsysteme“ includes „Erststimme“ and „Zweitstimme“, which are ballot/vote types rather than electoral systems. This overlaps with „Wahlprozess“ and „Wahl-Begriffe“.
findings.suggestedFix: Rename to „Stimmen bei der Wahl“ or replace with system terms only.

findings.explanation: „Gemeinderat“ and „Stadtrat“ can be offices, councils, or institutions depending on context. This overlaps with „Kommunale Institutionen“.
findings.suggestedFix: Use individual office holders only, e.g. „Bürgermeister, Kämmerer, Landrat, Beigeordneter“.

findings.explanation: „Grundsicherung“ is itself a social benefit type, not just a general social term, so it overlaps with „Sozialleistungs-Arten“.
findings.suggestedFix: Move „Grundsicherung“ to benefits or replace it with a clearer term like „Einkommensprüfung“.

findings.explanation: „Arztbesuch“, „Medikament“, „Krankenhaus“ and „Vorsorge“ are broad healthcare items, not specifically and cleanly „Kassenleistungen“. They overlap with general health-system vocabulary.
findings.suggestedFix: Use clearer benefit terms such as „Impfung“, „Check-up“, „Heilmittel“, „Zahnersatz“ or rename the group.

findings.explanation: „Krankenschein“ is dated vocabulary in Germany and no longer an everyday current health-system term for most contexts.
findings.suggestedFix: Use „Krankschreibung“ or „Arbeitsunfähigkeitsbescheinigung“.

findings.explanation: „Grundschuld“ is both an ownership/land-register concept and a financing security instrument, so it overlaps strongly with „Finanzierung“.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace „Grundschuld“ with a clearer ownership term such as „Flurstück“ or „Teilungserklärung“.

findings.explanation: „Mietvertrag“ is a central rental term and also the basis of tenant rights, so it overlaps with „Miet-Begriffe“.
findings.suggestedFix: Use a clearer tenant-rights item such as „Mietpreisbremse“ or „Kündigungsschutz“.

findings.explanation: „Servus“ is not exclusively Bavarian; it is also common in Austria and other southern German-speaking areas. It is still associated with Bavarian speech, but not uniquely.
findings.suggestedFix: Use a more specifically Bavarian item.

findings.explanation: „Tschüss“ is not specifically Plattdeutsch; it is widespread standard colloquial German. This makes the Plattdeutsch group less fair.
findings.suggestedFix: Use a more clearly Low German form.

findings.explanation: „Heim“ is standard German and not a clear Schwäbisch marker on its own.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace with a more recognizable Swabian word or phrase.

findings.explanation: Dialect-specific spellings such as „Drääsdner“, „Lütt“ and „Lüüd“ are niche for an easy puzzle unless the target audience is explicitly dialect-focused.
findings.suggestedFix: Move to medium or use very widely recognized dialect markers only.

findings.explanation: „Umarmung beim Tor“ can express joy, but it is a very specific sports-context scene and can also be a general gesture of comfort or solidarity. It is weaker than the other joy clues.
findings.suggestedFix: Use a clearer joy sign such as „Jubelruf“ or „Lachen vor Freude“.

findings.explanation: „Böllerschuss“ can signal celebration in some contexts, but it can also be associated with alarm, tradition, hunting or military salutes. It is not an everyday unambiguous sign of joy.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace with „Luftballons steigen“ or „Freudentanz“.

findings.explanation: „Tür steht immer offen“ is more hospitality/openness than direct freigiebigkeit. It could also indicate friendliness or trust rather than generosity.
findings.suggestedFix: Use a clearer giving expression such as „Teilt sein letztes Brot“.

findings.explanation: „Pestmaske“ is commonly associated with early modern plague doctors, not the medieval period in a clean historical sense.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace with a more clearly medieval item such as „Ritterrüstung“, „Klosterchronik“ or „Lehnsbrief“.

findings.explanation: „Schutznetz gespannt“ is avalanche protection infrastructure, not something that announces an avalanche. It does not match the category wording „Was Bergwetter ankündigt“.
findings.suggestedFix: Use a warning sign such as „Risse in der Schneedecke“ or „Wumm-Geräusch im Hang“.

findings.explanation: „Schneebruch im Wald“ is damage caused by snow load, not specifically an avalanche sign.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace with a direct avalanche-related clue.

findings.explanation: „Buttermilch gerührt“ is not a strong example of fermentation as a method in the same way as sauerkraut, bread dough and beer. It may read as churning/stirring dairy instead.
findings.suggestedFix: Use „Joghurt angesetzt“ or „Kimchi im Glas“.

findings.explanation: „Mut“ and „Entschlossenheit“ overlap conceptually in several clues. „Allein losgegangen“, „Kein Zurück mehr“, „Brücken abgebrochen“ and „Bis zum Ende durch“ all signal resolve/courage rather than cleanly separate categories.
findings.suggestedFix: Use more contrastive categories, or make „Mut“ physical/social courage and „Entschlossenheit“ decision-finality with clearer wording.

findings.explanation: The science concepts are high-school or specialist knowledge rather than concrete easy-tier everyday categories, especially quantum physics and relativity clues.
findings.suggestedFix: Move to medium/hard or simplify to everyday science categories.

findings.explanation: „Ehrlichkeit“ and „Loyalität“ overlap through promise-keeping and truthfulness. „Hält was er verspricht“ and „Steht zu seinem Wort“ can be read as honesty as much as loyalty.
findings.suggestedFix: Make loyalty clues more about allegiance/support, e.g. „Bleibt beim Team“ or „Verteidigt den Freund“.

findings.explanation: „Tanzend erzählt“ points to the original sense of ballad as dance song, but in German school/literary genre context it is not a clear defining feature of the literary ballad and may mislead.
findings.suggestedFix: Use a clearer ballad feature such as „Dramatische Handlung“ or „Strophenform“.

findings.explanation: „Unerhörte Begebenheit“, „Falke als Symbol“, „Wilhelm Meister“ and „Grüner Heinrich“ are literary-curriculum clues, not easy everyday categories.
findings.suggestedFix: Move to medium or use simpler genres such as „Märchen“, „Krimi“, „Gedicht“, „Theaterstück“.

findings.explanation: „Alle fliehen sofort“ can describe a rational emergency evacuation as well as mass panic. It is not as diagnostic as the other mass-panic items.
findings.suggestedFix: Use „Ausgänge verstopft“ or „Gedränge kippt um“.

findings.explanation: „Nein heißt vielleicht“ can imply manipulativeness or pressure rather than healthy persistence. It is a poor fairness clue for „Beharrlichkeit“.
findings.suggestedFix: Use „Nach Rückschlag weiter“ or „Täglich neu versucht“.

findings.explanation: „Satz zu Ende gesagt“ does not by itself express impatience; it could even signal patience or politeness. The intended phrase may be „Satz nicht zu Ende gehört“.
findings.suggestedFix: Change to „Satz nicht abgewartet“ or „Unterbricht sofort“.

findings.explanation: The puzzle has 17 items: the „Vertrauen“ group contains five entries, while a 4×4 puzzle requires exactly four items per group.
findings.suggestedFix: Remove one item from „Vertrauen“ so the puzzle has exactly 16 items.

findings.explanation: „Misstrauen“ and „Skepsis“ are closely overlapping concepts. „Immer nachgeprüft“, „Zeig mir den Beweis“, „Zweimal hingehört“ and „Quelle gefragt“ can all reasonably express skeptical verification.
findings.suggestedFix: Use more emotional distrust cues for „Misstrauen“ and evidence-checking cues for „Skepsis“, or replace one category.

findings.explanation: „Wiener Klassik“, „Volksmusik“ and „Kammermusik“ are not parallel-exclusive categories: chamber music is a form/ensemble setting that can exist within classical music, including the Wiener Klassik.
findings.suggestedFix: Use four parallel musical eras/genres, or replace „Kammermusik“ with another era/style.

findings.explanation: „Digitalisierung“ and „Vernetzung“ overlap strongly: „Fax stirbt“, „Akte wird Pixel“, „Erste Webseite online“, „Mail statt Brief“ and „Daten wandern global“ are all aspects of digital transformation and networked computing.
findings.suggestedFix: Make one group about digitizing analog processes and the other about internet/network infrastructure with very concrete non-overlapping clues.

findings.explanation: „Mobilität“ contains portable consumer devices, which overlaps with technological change and networking. „Handy in der Tasche“ and „Überall erreichbar“ especially fit networking as much as mobility.
findings.suggestedFix: Rename to „Mobile Geräte“ and make the items all device examples, or replace the category.

findings.explanation: „Hammer senken“ is an American courtroom image and not a normal German judge signal. For German-language easy puzzles, it can feel culturally imported.
findings.suggestedFix: Use „Robe tragen“, „Sitzung eröffnen“ or „Urteil unterschreiben“.

findings.explanation: Weather signs overlap between seasons: „Gewitter am Abend“ and „Gewitter nach Hitze“ are both summer-like thunderstorm clues, while „Erster Bodenfrost“ can also announce winter rather than only autumn.
findings.suggestedFix: Use more season-specific sky/weather signs for spring and autumn.

findings.explanation: „Stellt sich tot“ is not a characteristic fox behavior; it is more associated with animals such as opossums or some prey species. This makes the Fuchs group unfair.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace with a fox-specific clue such as „Schleicht im Bau“ or „Bellt heiser nachts“.

findings.explanation: „Schläft mit Augen auf“ is not a standard owl characteristic. Owls may appear watchful, but the clue is not reliable.
findings.suggestedFix: Use „Gewölle würgt aus“ or „Große Augen im Dunkeln“.

findings.explanation: The sports technique terms are quite specialized for easy tier, especially „Gabel“, „Zugzwang“, „Parade quarte“, „Riposte“ and several bowling/kegeln phrases.
findings.suggestedFix: Move to medium or use simpler, more everyday sport identifiers.

findings.explanation: „Bahn einölen“ is a bowling-lane maintenance idea, not a normal clue for German Kegeln in the same way as Kugel, Neun Kegel or Nachschub/Nachwurf.
findings.suggestedFix: Use „Kegel aufstellen“ or „Kugel rollen lassen“.

findings.explanation: „Kuppel über dem Schiff“ and „Doppelturm an Fassade“ are not uniquely Barock and can appear in other church architecture styles. The Barock group is less clean than the Romanik/Gotik groups.
findings.suggestedFix: Use more distinct Baroque clues such as „dramatische Lichtführung“, „Putten“, „bewegte Fassade“ or „illusionistische Decke“.

findings.explanation: „Herzklopfen, klingelt“ is malformed/awkward as an item. It reads like two fragments accidentally combined, not a clean clue.
findings.suggestedFix: Change to „Herz klopft schnell“ or „Herzklopfen beim Klingeln“.

findings.explanation: „Zuckertüte in der Hand“ is strongly associated with the first day of school, but it signals the event itself more than the emotion „Stolz“. It could also fit excitement.
findings.suggestedFix: Use a clearer pride clue such as „Brust raus beim Foto“.

findings.explanation: Several items have non-idiomatic German phrasing: „Seekrank am Reling“, „Karte ans Lenker“ and „Reiseführer gibt Mikro“ sound ungrammatical or clipped.
findings.suggestedFix: Use idiomatic forms such as „seekrank an der Reling“, „Karte am Lenker“ and „Reiseführer spricht ins Mikro“.

findings.explanation: „Schnittlinie anreißen“ is a measuring/marking action, not specific to a saw. It could belong broadly to many woodworking/tool tasks.
findings.suggestedFix: Use a saw-specific clue such as „Sägeblatt ansetzen“.

findings.explanation: „Kerfdruck“ is not a common German easy-tier term and may be a mistranslation/technical oddity. It makes the Säge group less accessible.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace with „gleichmäßig sägen“ or „Sägespäne fallen“.

findings.explanation: „Bit wechseln“ and „Kreuzklinge wechseln“ overlap because drill/driver bits and screwdriver tips are often interchangeable tool accessories. This can blur Bohrmaschine and Schraubenzieher.
findings.suggestedFix: Make the screwdriver clues manual-only, e.g. „Griff drehen“ or „Schraube lösen“.

findings.explanation: „Zeuge Jehovas“ as a doorbell visitor category is culturally recognizable but targets a religious group and can feel stereotyping compared with neutral occupational categories.
findings.suggestedFix: Use a neutral category such as „Nachbar“, „Spendensammler“ or „Umfrageperson“.

findings.explanation: „Klemmbrett dabei“ is not specific to museums; teachers or guides may carry a clipboard on any class trip. It is a weak museum clue.
findings.suggestedFix: Use „Vitrine betrachten“ or „Ausstellungsraum betreten“.

findings.explanation: „Pausensekt für Lehrer“ is not a student/class-trip theater feature and could be read as adult event behavior rather than theater itself.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace with „Sitzreihe suchen“ or „Applaus nach Schluss“.

findings.explanation: „Beerdigt Gefährten“ is not a clear, established raven-specific intelligence behavior. It may be confused with general animal mourning or corvid gatherings around dead birds.
findings.suggestedFix: Use a better-known raven/corvid intelligence clue such as „Werkzeug gebogen“ or „Futter versteckt“.

findings.explanation: „Malt mit Rüssel“ is a trained performance by captive elephants, not a natural sign of elephant intelligence. It is misleading as a category clue.
findings.suggestedFix: Use „Erkennt sich im Spiegel“ or „Trauert am Kadaver“.

findings.explanation: There are wording errors: „Carving am Blaupiste“ should be idiomatic German such as „Carving auf der blauen Piste“, and „Halftube“ should be „Halfpipe“.
findings.suggestedFix: Correct the German phrasing and terminology.

findings.explanation: „Kante wechseln“ applies to both skiing and snowboarding, so it is not specific enough for the Snowboard group.
findings.suggestedFix: Use a snowboard-specific clue such as „Board quer stellen“ or „Eine Planke unter den Füßen“.

findings.explanation: „Flug in der Nacht“ is not specific to a family with child; business travelers, backpackers and seniors can also take night flights. It is a weak category clue.
findings.suggestedFix: Use „Buggy am Gate“ or „Kinderwagen aufgeben“.

findings.explanation: „Handy nie weg“ is not specific to Geschäftsreisender and can apply to most traveler types.
findings.suggestedFix: Use a business-specific clue such as „Laptop im Handgepäck“ or „Meeting nach Landung“.

findings.explanation: „Österreich“ and „Bayern“ overlap culturally and culinarily, especially around Semmel/Brezel-style breakfast items and southern German/Austrian terms. The split is not clean enough for easy tier.
findings.suggestedFix: Use more distinct regions or more uniquely diagnostic breakfast items.

findings.explanation: Several clues are awkward or not idiomatic: „Haferflocken alt“ and „Korn dazu eigentlich“ are especially unclear as standalone German items.
findings.suggestedFix: Rewrite as idiomatic clues, e.g. „Haferflocken über Nacht“ and, if intended, use a clearer North German breakfast item.
